jokes.text: deanel-ganger
jokes.setup: Character corrects 'doppel-deaner' with an even sillier portmanteau
jokes.punchline: The absurd compound word 'deanel-ganger'
jokes.explanation: Clever Dean-based wordplay that's both economical and original, though not deeply character-specific

jokes.text: doppel-deaner
jokes.setup: Need to describe the Dean's replacement
jokes.punchline: Dean-specific twist on 'doppelganger'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay but more predictable than 'deanel-ganger'

jokes.text: ♪ Love is not admissible evidence ♪
jokes.setup: Character responds to cop's legal statement
jokes.punchline: Breaking into song about legal technicalities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Community absurdist moment - character randomly breaking into song about mundane legal concept

jokes.text: I'm working on a cop opera. Cop-ra! Police-ical!
jokes.setup: Character explains their musical project
jokes.punchline: Two increasingly elaborate puns on opera
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from cop-ra to police-ical, showing comedic commitment

jokes.text: Wait, cop-ra!
jokes.setup: Another character suddenly remembers the pun
jokes.punchline: Delayed appreciation for the wordplay
jokes.explanation: Nice callback beat showing the pun was worth remembering

jokes.text: It's a prescription throne. I have back mumps. Don't look it up. It's rare.
jokes.setup: Chang needs to justify his expensive throne
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up medical condition with confident delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang absurdity - confident delivery of completely fabricated medical excuse. 'Don't look it up' is brilliant

jokes.text: Fire the custodial staff. Okay. Cool.
jokes.setup: Chang needs to save money for his party
jokes.punchline: Casually firing entire department, aide's unfazed response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chang's casual cruelty and aide's Stockholm syndrome

jokes.text: They're like... Telephones for your eyes.
jokes.setup: Character tries to explain binoculars poetically
jokes.punchline: Hilariously literal and unhelpful metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly stupid metaphor delivered with complete sincerity. Very quotable

jokes.text: What kind of monster would... Photo booth with props!
jokes.setup: Character is outraged by Chang's party plans
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by actually appealing party feature
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection - character's moral outrage derailed by party envy

jokes.text: Oh, no, Jeffrey. Looks like PB and J again.
jokes.setup: AC guy is describing serious surveillance situation
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about someone's lunch like it's equally important
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating mundane lunch observation as surveillance intel

jokes.text: a 4'10", 105-pound bucket of piss and zit cream. I cannot express to you how much I hate this kid.
jokes.setup: Describing Chang's lieutenant Joshua
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and disgusting description followed by passionate hatred
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific insult with great character voice. The passionate hatred makes it funnier

jokes.text: beyond that dog sneeze in a pair of reeboks
jokes.setup: Continuing description of Joshua
jokes.punchline: Another creatively disgusting metaphor
jokes.explanation: Another brilliant insult from the AC guy. 'Dog sneeze in reeboks' is memorably gross

jokes.text: Not a lot of people get a second chance. Just you and probably Obama.
jokes.setup: Making a point about second chances
jokes.punchline: Casually comparing Troy to the President
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan comparison. The casual 'probably Obama' is brilliantly understated

jokes.text: Do you know how long someone as sarcastic as I am would last in prison? Such a long time.
jokes.setup: Character worried about prison survival
jokes.punchline: Uses sarcasm to demonstrate exactly why they'd have problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-demonstrating joke - character proves their point by being sarcastic about being sarcastic

jokes.text: Everyone except Pierce has to plan an elaborate heist.
jokes.setup: Pierce wants to do his swami act
jokes.punchline: Specifically excluding Pierce from the competent plan
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific exclusion that acknowledges Pierce's incompetence

jokes.text: Shower organizer? Are you guys psychic?
jokes.setup: School board gives Chang a birthday gift
jokes.punchline: Chang is genuinely excited about mundane bathroom organizer
jokes.explanation: Shows Chang's weird priorities and genuine enthusiasm for odd gifts

jokes.text: I won't have them ruining my 25th birthday.
jokes.setup: Chang giving security instructions
jokes.punchline: Chang claiming to be 25 years old
jokes.explanation: Chang's delusional age claim fits his character's vanity and self-deception

jokes.text: You had time to build a tiny working water fountain, and I'm a pinecone?
jokes.setup: Character sees elaborate heist planning model
jokes.punchline: Complaining about being represented as a pinecone while appreciating the detail
jokes.explanation: Great visual contrast between elaborate model details and lazy character representation

jokes.text: This girl in Chicago won't stop facebooking me. Looks like j-swag's going deep dish.
jokes.setup: Character describing online attention
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun connecting Facebook stalking to Chicago pizza
jokes.explanation: Awkward pun that's funny because the character thinks it's clever

jokes.text: Sounds like you schnooks got a flood the size of my wife's attitude problem.
jokes.setup: Fake plumber assessing the situation
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical 'my wife' joke delivered in character
jokes.explanation: Cliched 'wife' joke but works as character acting choice for the disguise

jokes.text: Boys are pathetic. Two days of facebooking with this Joshua kid and he's eating out of my hands.
jokes.setup: Annie describing her manipulation of Joshua
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of manipulating a child online
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about online manipulation with Annie's ruthless efficiency

jokes.text: You people gotta start chewin' your food. ... Like my wife's mouth.
jokes.setup: Continuing the plumber character performance
jokes.punchline: Another stereotypical plumber 'my wife' complaint
jokes.explanation: Repetitive wife joke that's getting stale, though maintains character consistency

jokes.text: Got a new flavor of lip balm. Meet me at Chili's in ten minutes." Sheep.
jokes.setup: Character explaining how they're catfishing Joshua
jokes.punchline: Absurdly mundane bait that somehow works
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail - lip balm as irresistible bait perfectly captures Joshua's patheticness

jokes.text: "A," that is racist. "B," swamis can't drive... they're Indians.
jokes.setup: Pierce defending his swami act
jokes.punchline: Calls something racist then immediately says something more racist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - shows his complete lack of self-awareness about his own racism

jokes.text: I'll kill anyone who raises their hand.
jokes.setup: Magician asking for volunteer
jokes.punchline: Chang threatens murder over party participation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly escalated Chang aggression - turns party magic into genuine threat

jokes.text: Well, everybody else got to say it.
jokes.setup: Multiple characters have said 'I'm in' during heist
jokes.punchline: Character wants their moment to say the catchphrase too
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment acknowledging the heist movie cliche the show just repeated

jokes.text: Is this your card? Huh? I don't remember.
jokes.setup: Classic magic trick reveal
jokes.punchline: Chang genuinely doesn't remember his card, ruining the trick
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of magic trick expectations - Chang's genuine confusion deflates the moment

jokes.text: No, not knowing this Pierce. I... I am a mystical swami. I come to boogie down, I'm boogying down.
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to maintain his swami disguise when recognized
jokes.punchline: Terrible accent and awkward dancing while denying identity
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce incompetence - his terrible disguise and commitment to the bit despite being caught

jokes.text: Negative, the pixie has left the toadstool.
jokes.setup: Guard reporting Dean's escape over radio
jokes.punchline: Using fairy tale code names for surveillance subjects
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd code names that sound like fairy tale references in serious tactical situation

jokes.text: Hey, Johnny Passerby, this job's getting bigger than my wife's credit card bill at Marshalls. I mean, they got great stuff, I know that.
jokes.setup: Fake plumber talking to guard
jokes.punchline: Another wife complaint that gets sidetracked into genuine store endorsement
jokes.explanation: Slight variation on the wife joke pattern with unexpected genuine product endorsement

jokes.text: Oof baboof, I spilled plumber's acid all over yous.
jokes.setup: Abed creating distraction
jokes.punchline: Uses the nonsense 'oof baboof' phrase again plus 'plumber's acid'
jokes.explanation: Good callback of nonsense phrase plus invented 'plumber's acid' as plot device

jokes.text: Sar-cas-tic claps.
jokes.setup: Chang doing slow clap for captured group
jokes.punchline: Verbally announcing his own sarcastic clapping
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment - Chang announcing his own sarcasm defeats the purpose perfectly

jokes.text: You think you caught us, but we've already made it out with the real Dean. Ocean's eleven, baby. Pierce, I'm the real Dean.
jokes.setup: Character claiming clever heist success
jokes.punchline: Pierce immediately ruins it by talking to the Dean they supposedly rescued
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of heist movie bravado by Pierce's obliviousness

jokes.text: Getting caught the first time was part of the plan. This time is because we suck.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to maintain dignity after failed plan
jokes.punchline: Admits complete failure with deadpan honesty
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan admission of incompetence - Jeff's ego deflated into honesty

jokes.text: Shirley, where the hell did you come from?
jokes.setup: Shirley appears unexpectedly in the captured group
jokes.punchline: No one knows how she got there since she wasn't caught with them
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about inexplicable character appearance - raises questions about heist logistics

jokes.text: Fire can't go through doors, stupid. It's not a ghost.
jokes.setup: Group worried about Chang burning down the school
jokes.punchline: Chang's completely wrong understanding of how fire works
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Chang logic - comparing fire to ghosts with complete confidence. Perfectly quotable and absurd

jokes.text: As many of you know, I ate my twin sister in utero. So on my birthday, I try to remember I'm a winner.
jokes.setup: Chang giving birthday speech
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of cannibalism framed as motivational backstory
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark humor delivered with Chang's characteristic matter-of-fact insanity

jokes.text: This one's for you, Connie. And a-one, a-two, a Chang, Chang, Chang-four.
jokes.setup: Chang dedicating his performance to his eaten twin
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'three' with his name in count-off
jokes.explanation: Consistent character narcissism - can't even count without inserting his name

jokes.text: They call it "academania." It's mostly hard house, some... some dubstep, but totally...
jokes.setup: Dean explaining his rave rental business
jokes.punchline: Dean trying to sound cool about electronic music genres
jokes.explanation: Good Dean characterization - his attempt to be hip with rave culture while being fundamentally uncool

jokes.text: You weigh nothing! - I can't see them. They're too many of them. - Oof. - Baboof.
jokes.setup: Fight scene chaos
jokes.punchline: Absurd sound effects and contradictory observations during combat
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier nonsense phrase, good for physical comedy but not exceptional

jokes.text: It is hard out there for a fake Moby!
jokes.setup: Fake Dean being arrested
jokes.punchline: Comparing his plight to rapper/DJ Moby with 'It's Hard Out Here for a Pimp' reference
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture mash-up combining Moby with Oscar-winning song reference

jokes.text: Carl, Richie, let me ex-Chang.
jokes.setup: Chang wants to explain to the school board
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'explain' with 'ex-Chang'
jokes.explanation: Consistent character trait - Chang's compulsive need to insert his name into words

jokes.text: I'm not getting paid, am I?
jokes.setup: Dramatic confrontation and arrest
jokes.punchline: Fake Dean's practical concern about compensation
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax - deflates dramatic moment with mundane workplace concern

jokes.text: Well, I have a drinking problem.
jokes.setup: Board members reflecting on how things went wrong
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of alcoholism as explanation
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny casual admission of personal problem

jokes.text: I do it for Greendale. Aw.
jokes.setup: Dean's noble speech about taking responsibility
jokes.punchline: Group's immediate 'aw' response treating it like cute rather than heroic
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of serious moment with patronizing group response

jokes.text: Never wear a rubber.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving parting advice to Troy
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual advice disguised as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Pierce advice that's economical but predictable

jokes.text: Never listen to Pierce.
jokes.setup: Immediately following Pierce's advice
jokes.punchline: Direct contradiction/warning about Pierce's counsel
jokes.explanation: Quick, economical callback that reinforces Pierce's reputation for bad advice

jokes.text: Never listen to Pierce.
jokes.setup: Pierce's inappropriate advice to Troy
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately contradicts Pierce's advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Jeff's immediate damage control after Pierce's advice

jokes.text: This is a lock of my hair. Creepy.
jokes.setup: Character giving sentimental goodbye gift
jokes.punchline: Recipient immediately identifies it as creepy
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of sentimental moment with blunt honesty about the weirdness

jokes.text: He said, "I know you hate when people do this in movies."
jokes.setup: Character asking what Abed said during emotional goodbye
jokes.punchline: Abed acknowledging the cliche of emotional movie moments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary - acknowledging emotional manipulation while participating in it
